description: Complete overhaul of Wizrails from 3D track builder to 2D pixel-art retro train simulator on the New Delhi to Chandigarh Shatabdi Express route. See prompt.md for full design doc.

userStories.title: Strip 3D dependencies and set up 2D canvas foundation
userStories.description: Remove all React Three Fiber, drei, Three.js, and postprocessing dependencies. Set up a Canvas 2D rendering system with a game loop using requestAnimationFrame. Keep React for UI overlays, Zustand for state, and the Rust/WASM setup. The canvas should render at 480x270 base resolution and CSS scale up with image-rendering: pixelated.
acceptanceCriteria: All @react-three/* and three and postprocessing packages removed from package.json
All 3D component files removed (Terrain.tsx, TrackGrid.tsx, Trees.tsx, etc.)
New GameCanvas component renders a <canvas> element at 480x270 scaled to fill viewport
Game loop runs via requestAnimationFrame with delta time calculation
Canvas renders a test rectangle to prove it works
image-rendering: pixelated CSS applied to canvas
npm run build passes with zero errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create new Zustand game store for 2D train sim
userStories.description: Replace the existing 3D-oriented store with a new one for the train simulator. Must include: route data (5 stations with km positions and arrival times), train state (position in km, speed, throttle, braking), game clock (starts at 17:15), game phase (menu/running/station-stop/results), signal state, and reputation score.
acceptanceCriteria: store.ts fully rewritten with new GameState interface
Route data includes all 5 stations: New Delhi (km 0), Panipat (km 90), Kurukshetra (km 157), Ambala (km 199), Chandigarh (km 266)
Each station has name, code, km position, scheduled arrival time, halt duration
Train state: positionKm, speedKmh, throttle (0-1), isBraking, maxSpeed
Game clock with currentTime (starts 17:15), timeScale for fast-forward
Signal state type defined (red/yellow/green)
Reputation score and passenger ratings array
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement parallax scrolling background system
userStories.description: Build a multi-layer parallax scrolling renderer on the canvas. 4 layers: far sky/clouds, mid buildings/fields/landmarks, near trackside elements (poles, signals, fences), and foreground (tracks/train). Each layer scrolls at different speeds relative to train speed. The background content changes based on current km position on the route.
acceptanceCriteria: ParallaxRenderer class that draws 4 layers at different scroll speeds
Layers tile/repeat seamlessly as train moves
Scroll speed proportional to actual train speed from store
Background changes based on positionKm (different scenery zones)
At least 2 distinct zone visuals working: urban Delhi (grey, buildings) and farmland (green/golden fields)
Sky gradient changes over time (golden afternoon → sunset → dusk)
Renders correctly on the 480x270 canvas
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render pixel-art train exterior and tracks
userStories.description: Draw a pixel-art Shatabdi Express train on the foreground layer. The train stays roughly centered horizontally while the background scrolls. Show railway tracks beneath the train. The train should have a recognizable Indian Railways look: blue-and-cream livery with the distinctive Shatabdi stripes.
acceptanceCriteria: Pixel art train drawn on canvas — locomotive + 2-3 visible carriages
Train has blue body, cream/white stripe, and visible windows
Railway tracks (two rails + wooden ties) render beneath the train
Train has subtle animation: wheels turning relative to speed, slight bounce
Smoke/exhaust particle pixels from locomotive when moving
Train stays centered while background scrolls
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build HUD control panel with speed and signal controls
userStories.description: Create the bottom control panel as a React overlay (not canvas). Include: throttle slider (0-100%), brake button, current speed display (km/h), speed limit indicator, next signal color and distance, next station name and ETA, mini-map (line with station dots and current position), and in-game clock.
acceptanceCriteria: Control panel renders as a fixed React overlay at bottom of screen
Throttle slider controls train speed (0% to 100%)
Brake button that decelerates the train
Speed display shows current speed in km/h
Speed limit indicator shows current zone max speed
Signal indicator shows next signal color (red/yellow/green circle)
Next station info: name, distance, ETA
Mini-map line showing all 5 stations with current position marker
In-game clock displaying current time (HH:MM format)
Retro pixel-style font and bordered panel aesthetic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement train physics and speed management
userStories.description: Wire up the throttle and brake to actual train movement. Train accelerates based on throttle, decelerates on brake. Speed is clamped to the current zone's speed limit. Train position advances based on speed. Implement speed zones: 130 km/h max on open track, 60 km/h through station areas, auto-slow approach to stations.
acceptanceCriteria: Train accelerates smoothly when throttle > 0
Train decelerates when brake is applied
Train speed is clamped to current speed zone limit
Position (km) advances based on speed and delta time
Speed zones defined: 130 km/h open track, 60 km/h near stations (within 2 km)
Overspeeding in restricted zone shows visual warning
Train stops at 0 km/h when braking from any speed
Game clock advances proportionally (3h15m journey maps to real route)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement station stops with boarding/alighting
userStories.description: When the train reaches a station's km position and is stopped (speed=0), enter station-stop mode. Show a station platform view on canvas with pixel-art station building and platform. Display a halt timer counting down. Animated pixel NPCs walk on/off the train. Player must depart before timer expires or lose reputation.
acceptanceCriteria: Game detects arrival at station when train is within 0.5 km and speed < 5 km/h
Station-stop mode activates: background changes to station platform scene
Each station has a distinct pixel-art building matching real station character
Halt timer counts down (2 min for Panipat/Kurukshetra, 3 min for Ambala)
Pixel NPC sprites walk onto and off the train during halt
Depart button appears; must be clicked to resume journey
Late departure (after halt timer) shows penalty notification
Early departure is allowed but passengers may not all board
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build signal system with block sections
userStories.description: Place signals along the route at realistic intervals (~every 5-10 km). Signals cycle between green/yellow/red based on simulated traffic. Player must respond: green = full speed, yellow = slow to 60 km/h, red = stop completely. Running a red signal triggers an accident/game-over. Show signal visuals on the near-background parallax layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Signals placed every 5-10 km along the 266 km route
Each signal has a state: green, yellow, or red
Signals rendered as pixel-art posts with colored lights in the near-background layer
Upcoming signal color and distance shown in HUD
Green: no restriction. Yellow: speed limit 60 km/h. Red: must stop
Passing a red signal at speed > 5 km/h triggers accident event
Accident shows game-over screen with option to restart
Signals change based on simple simulated block occupancy (random other trains)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rich scenery for Delhi-Panipat segment
userStories.description: Fill in detailed pixel-art scenery for the Delhi to Panipat segment (km 0-90). This must feel authentic: Delhi rail yards with parked trains → Yamuna river bridge (wide sandy floodplain) → brick kilns with chimneys → endless wheat and mustard fields (golden/yellow) → GT Road with trucks → canal crossings → IOC Panipat Refinery (industrial skyline with flare stacks).
acceptanceCriteria: Km 0-10: Delhi urban — apartment blocks, overhead wires, metro viaducts, rail yards
Km 10-20: Yamuna crossing — wide sandy/green floodplain, bridge structure
Km 20-50: Agricultural plains — wheat fields (golden), mustard fields (yellow flowers), brick kilns with tall chimneys
Km 50-70: GT Road visible parallel — trucks, buses, tractors as pixel sprites
Km 70-85: Canal crossings, more fields, small villages with flat-roofed houses
Km 85-90: Panipat Refinery — industrial flare stacks, storage tanks, distillation towers
All scenery renders as pixel art on parallax layers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rich scenery for Panipat-Kurukshetra segment
userStories.description: Detailed pixel-art scenery for km 90-157. Greener irrigated farmland → sugarcane fields → Markanda River → tube-well pump houses → larger villages → dharamshalas and ashrams appearing near Kurukshetra. Sunset lighting begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Km 90-110: Greener farmland, more irrigated look, sugarcane fields (tall green walls)
Km 110-130: Markanda River crossing (seasonal sandy bed), villages with brick houses
Km 130-150: Tube-well pump houses, larger villages, dharamshalas/ashrams
Km 150-157: Kurukshetra approach — temple spires, religious signage, pilgrimage town feel
Sky color transitions toward sunset (warm orange tones)
All scenery renders as pixel art on parallax layers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rich scenery for Kurukshetra-Ambala-Chandigarh segment
userStories.description: Detailed pixel-art scenery for km 157-266. Reddish-brown soil → Ghaggar River → more trees (neem, eucalyptus) → military cantonment near Ambala → undulating terrain → Shivalik Hills on horizon → Chandigarh's modern boulevards. Full sunset to dusk transition.
acceptanceCriteria: Km 157-180: Reddish-brown soil, Ghaggar River crossing, mango/neem groves
Km 180-199: Military cantonment buildings, barracks, ordnance depots, eucalyptus plantations
Km 199-230: Terrain becomes undulating (subtle elevation changes in background)
Km 230-260: Shivalik Hills visible on far horizon (blue-green mountain silhouette), denser vegetation
Km 260-266: Chandigarh — wide boulevards, organized sectors, modern buildings, greenery
Sky fully transitions from sunset to dusk (deep blue with city lights)
All scenery renders as pixel art on parallax layers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Train interior cross-section view
userStories.description: Add a toggleable train interior view showing a side-view cross-section of the Shatabdi carriages. Player can see seats, pantry car, vestibule areas, and windows showing scenery. This is the management view where the player can interact with the train.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button switches between exterior/interior view
Interior shows side-view cross-section of 2-3 carriages
Seats visible in AC Chair Car layout (2+2 rows)
Pantry car section identifiable
Windows show the same parallax scenery as exterior view
Pixel-art interior details: seat cushions, luggage racks, aisle
View scrolls horizontally to see different carriages
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NPC passenger system with boarding and preferences
userStories.description: Implement NPC passengers that board at each station. Each NPC has a type (Business, Family, Pilgrim, Student, Army Officer, Food Blogger, Tourist), visible as a distinct pixel sprite, and carries preferences that affect their rating. NPCs sit in seats and have mood indicators.
acceptanceCriteria: 7 NPC types defined with distinct pixel-art sprites (at least 8x16 px each)
NPCs board at appropriate stations (e.g., Pilgrim at Kurukshetra, Army at Ambala)
Each NPC type has defined preferences (punctuality weight, food weight, comfort weight, etc.)
NPCs appear in seats in the interior view
Mood indicator shown above each NPC (happy/neutral/unhappy pixel icon)
Passenger manifest panel shows who's onboard and their type
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rating and scoring system
userStories.description: At the end of the journey (Chandigarh arrival) and during the journey, NPCs rate their experience. Calculate ratings based on punctuality, speed management, signal obedience, food service, and comfort. Show a journey results screen with breakdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Each NPC calculates a 1-5 star rating based on their weighted preferences
Punctuality factor: on-time arrivals at each station boost rating
Safety factor: signal violations, overspeeding, hard braking reduce rating
Overall reputation score displayed in HUD (average of all ratings)
Journey results screen at Chandigarh: total score, per-category breakdown, per-NPC ratings
Results show: punctuality %, fuel efficiency, passenger satisfaction, safety record
Option to play again from results screen
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Random events system
userStories.description: Implement random events that occur during the journey to keep gameplay dynamic. Events include: cattle on tracks, fog patches, signal failures, chain pulling, medical emergencies. Each requires a player response.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 distinct random events implemented
Cattle on tracks: must brake hard, visual of cow on tracks
Fog patch: reduced visibility (darken/blur parallax), must slow down
Signal failure: must proceed at restricted speed (15 km/h)
Chain pulling: train auto-stops, player investigates (timer to resume)
Medical emergency: popup with choice — stop at next station or call ahead
Events trigger at random intervals (not too frequent, not too rare)
Each event affects score: good response = neutral/bonus, bad response = penalty
Visual notification/popup for each event
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Start menu and game flow polish
userStories.description: Add a start screen with pixel-art title, a pre-departure phase at New Delhi where the player can review their train, and smooth transitions between all game phases. Include a how-to-play tutorial overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Start screen: 'WIZRAILS' pixel-art title with animated train, 'Start Journey' button
Pre-departure phase at New Delhi: overview of train, passenger manifest preview
Smooth fade transitions between game phases
How-to-play overlay explaining controls (throttle, brake, signals, stations)
Pause menu (Esc key) with Resume / Restart / Quit options
Game properly resets on restart
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update Rust/WASM engine for train sim
userStories.description: Update the Rust WASM module: remove terrain generation, add signal logic (block section occupancy), scheduling calculations (ETA, delay, punctuality score), and NPC rating calculation. Expose new functions via wasm_bindgen.
acceptanceCriteria: Old terrain.rs and pathfinder.rs removed
New signals.rs: check_signal(position, speed, block_states) -> signal state
New scheduler.rs: calculate_eta(position, speed, target_km) -> arrival time
New scheduler.rs: punctuality_score(scheduled, actual) -> score
New npc.rs: calculate_rating(preferences, journey_stats) -> rating
All functions exported via wasm_bindgen
wasm-pack build --target web succeeds
Frontend imports and uses at least one new WASM function
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Audio system with train sounds
userStories.description: Add a basic audio system. Generate or load retro-style sound effects: train chugging (speed-dependent), whistle at departures, signal bell, station announcements (simple chime), and event notification sounds.
acceptanceCriteria: Audio manager class that handles playing/stopping sounds
Train chugging sound that changes pitch/speed with train speed
Whistle sound plays at station departure
Bell/chime sound at station arrival
Alert sound for random events and signal warnings
Master volume control accessible from pause menu
Sounds generated programmatically (Web Audio API oscillators) for retro feel
Audio can be muted/unmuted
Typecheck passes
